source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ FDA/기술문서/Usability Engineering/09-01. Usability Engineering Report (Doc.No.A-TCF-5-056(rev.0).docx

document.title: Usability Engineering Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector
standards: EN 60601-1-6:2010 Medical electrical equipment Part 1-6: General requirements for safety – Collateral standard: Usability
IEC 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2013 Medical electrical equipment Part 1-6: General requirements for safety – Collateral standard: Usability
EN 62366 [2008] Application of usability engineering to medical devices
IEC 62366:2007+A1:2014 Application of usability engineering to medical devices
EN ISO 14971[2012] Medical devices – Application of risk management to medical devices
ISO 14971 [2007] Medical devices -- Application of risk management to medical devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability engineering report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector documenting safety-related usability process, application specification, hazards, UI requirements, verification, validation references, and conclusion.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports the usability engineering file for ADD by recording analysis, specification, design, verification and validation activities related to safety of normal use and use errors.
keyPoints: ADD is indicated for digital imaging solution for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
The intended use environment is hospital, professional use, with operation temperature 5℃ to 40℃ and humidity 30 to 75% relative humidity.
Frequently used functions include cable connections, powering on the detector, patient positioning, and detector calibration by PC software.
Known or foreseeable usability-related hazardous situations include PC calibration operation, electromagnetic environment influence, user control error, indicator error, and battery use error.
Usability verification results for UI-01 through UI-07 are recorded as No issues and Pass.
The conclusion states residual risk can be accepted after hazard identification and risk control measures.

concepts.name: Usability engineering process
concepts.evidence: This document specifies a process for a manufacturer to analyse, specify, design, verify and validate usability, as it relates to safety of.

concepts.name: Normal use and use errors
concepts.evidence: This usability engineering process assesses and mitigates risks caused by usability problems associated with correct use and use errors, i.e. NORMAL USE.

concepts.name: Application specification
concepts.evidence: Application specification - Description, intended use - Medical purpose - Intended PATIENT population - Intended part of the body or type of tissue applied to or interacted with - Intended user profile - Intended conditions of use - Operating principle

concepts.name: Primary operating functions
concepts.evidence: Function that involves USER interaction that is either frequently used or related to the SAFETY of the MEDICAL DEVICE.

concepts.name: Usability verification
concepts.evidence: Shall verify the implementation of the medical device user interface design against the requirements of the usability specification.

concepts.name: Usability validation
concepts.evidence: Shall validate usability of the medical device according to the usability validation plan.

concepts.name: Risk control priority
concepts.evidence: Risk control uses one or more of the following in the priority listed: a) inherent safety by design; b) protective measures in the medical device itself or in the manufacturing process, e.g. alarm systems; c) Information for safety

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz

evidence.claim: The product name is Abyz Digital Detector and the model name is ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name: Abyz Digital Detector Model Name: ADD

evidence.claim: The report references IEC 60601-1-6, IEC 62366, and ISO 14971 usability and risk management standards.
ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children... Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The intended patient population includes radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, chest, spinal, abdominal, hand, foot, and internal organs.
evidence.evidenceText: Radiographic imaging of human body parts such as head, Chest, spinal, abdominal, particular, hand, foot and other internal organs.

evidence.claim: The intended user experience profile identifies radiologist experience.
evidence.evidenceText: d) Experience: Radiologist

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for professional hospital use.
evidence.evidenceText: Environment: General Hospital, intended for professional use

evidence.claim: ADD operating conditions are 5℃ to 40℃, 30 to 75% relative humidity, and 700 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Operation condition Temperature: 5℃ to 40℃ Humidity: 30 to 75% relative humidity (non-condensing) Air Pressure: 700 hPa to 1060 hPa

evidence.claim: ADD storage conditions are -10℃ to 50℃, 10 to 80% relative humidity, and 500 hPa to 1060 hPa.
evidence.evidenceText: Storage condition Temperature: -10℃ to 50℃ Humidity: 10 to 80% relative humidity (non-condensing) Air Pressure: 500 hPa to 1060 hPa

evidence.claim: Frequently used functions include power and cable connections, powering on the detector, positioning the patient, and detector calibration by PC software.
evidence.evidenceText: Connecting the power cord to the power supply... Power on the detector Arrange the patient in the correct position... Calibration of detector by S/W in PC

evidence.claim: The device is used with X-ray generator, PACS, and MWL according to the safety characteristics checklist.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.3 Is the Medical device intended for use in conjunction with medicines or other medical technologies? A X-ray device (Generator) Picture Archiving and Communication System (PACS) Modality Worklist (MWL)

evidence.claim: ADD requires periodic calibration.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.7 Is maintenance and/or calibration necessary A The ADD will need periodic calibration.

evidence.claim: ADD requires professional use.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.12 Does installation or use of the Medical device require special training or special skills? A Need professional use

evidence.claim: Information for safe use is provided by user manual and label.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.13 How will information for safe use be provided? A Provided user manual and label

evidence.claim: ADD is identified as a portable device.
evidence.evidenceText: E.2.22 Is the Medical device intended to be mobile or portable? A Portable device

evidence.claim: Usability-related hazard IDs include UR-01 PC calibration operation, UR-02 electromagnetic working environment influence, UR-03 user control error, UR-04 indicator error, and UR-05 battery use error.
evidence.evidenceText: UR-01 Other tasks which can interfere with the operation of the MD A ADD can be operated with PC for calibration... UR-02 Influence on the working environment A MEE generates electromagnetic wave... UR-03 Product context User control error A... UR-04 Indicator error A... UR-05 Battery use error A

evidence.claim: Safety-related functions include detector and power supply power buttons and LED indication.
evidence.evidenceText: Functions related to Safety a) Detector / Power supply - Power On/off button - LED indication

evidence.claim: Battery remaining volume is represented by LED indicators for battery status ranges.
evidence.evidenceText: b) Battery - Battery remaining volume - If the battery is not charging Indicator Battery status LED 1, 2, 3 and 4 on 76% to 100%

evidence.claim: Reasonably foreseeable worst case use scenarios include wiring disconnection, inattention before X-ray exposures, wrong wiring, dropping, excessive force, wrong calibration, high temperature, and water/humidity.
evidence.evidenceText: Wiring disconnection to Detector => no reading... Wrong wiring of interface cable from X-ray machine to Detector Dropping Applying excessive force Wrong calibration in the control software Environment High temperature => failure of function Water/humidity => failure of electronic/electric shock

evidence.claim: User interface requirements include user-friendly instructions, instructions for powering on, checking operation, cleaning, storage, simple use, inability to control output, and indicators.
evidence.evidenceText: Write user-friendly, easy-to-understand instructions... Add the instructions how to turn on the device... check the operation... clean... storage... Simple use method Can’t control output Add the indicator

evidence.claim: The usability validation plan is referenced as Doc. No. A-TCF-5-039.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Plan Refer to Usability Validation Plan (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-039)

evidence.claim: The usability validation report is referenced as Doc. No. A-TCF-5-040.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability Validation Refer to Usability Validation Report (Doc. No. A-TCF-5-040)

evidence.claim: Usability verification results for UI-01 through UI-07 are all No issues and Pass.
evidence.evidenceText: UI-01 No issues Pass UI-02 No issues Pass UI-03 No issues Pass UI-04 No issues Pass UI-05 No issues Pass UI-06 No issues Pass UI-07 No issues Pass

evidence.claim: The report concludes that overall residual risk can be accepted.
evidence.evidenceText: Through overall residual risk assessment, we are confirmed that overall of the residual risk can be accepted.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is named in the source text, despite source metadata authoritySignal indicating MFDS/domestic.
Source metadata productSignal states HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the document text identifies product/model as Abyz Digital Detector / ADD.
Document contains OCR or source-text quality issues such as 'Person suing', 'Men by which', duplicated list lettering, and inconsistent A/N/A comments.
The document references usability validation plan/report document numbers A-TCF-5-039 and A-TCF-5-040 but their contents are not included in this source.
